item.title: The haunting ruins of Khara Khoto,also known as the "Black City," lie in western Inner Mongolia, China. Founded in 1032, this city thrived as a trading hub along the Silk Road for centuries.
item.description: The haunting ruins of Khara Khoto, also known as the "Black City," lie in western Inner Mongolia, China. Founded in 1032, this city thrived as a trading hub along the Silk Road for centuries. However, by the late 14th century, it was mysteriously abandoned, possibly due to a diverted water source or warfare. Buried by sand for centuries, Khara Khoto was rediscovered in the early 20th century, revealing artifacts like religious texts, artwork, and everyday objects.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.dc:creator: softspokensatan

item.title: A Ferocious Dog
item.description: Gary Paulsen photo By Wyoming Department of Education, CC BY-SA 3.0, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=111023264 https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/3.0/ Sauce: Edith Hope Fine, Gary Paulsen: Author and Wilderness Adventurer (Berkeley Heights, NJ: Enslow Publications, Inc., 2000), pp. 38-39. “Gary James Paulsen (May 17, 1939 – October 13, 2021) was an American writer of children's and young adult fiction, best known for coming-of-age stories about the wilderness. He was the author of more than 200 books and wrote more than 200 magazine articles and short stories, and several plays, all primarily for teenagers. He won the Margaret Edwards Award from the American Library Association in 1997 for his lifetime contribution in writing for teens. […] “Bits and pieces of Gary's adolescence can be cobbled together in Guts: The True Stories Behind Hatchet and the Brian Books. In that book, Paulsen discusses how he survived between the ages of twelve and fourteen back in Minnesota. He barely mentions his parents except to say that they were too busy being drunk to stock the refrigerator. He worked several jobs during this time, including setting pins at a bowling alley, delivering newspapers, and working as a farmhand. He bought his own school supplies and a .22 single-shot rifle, which he used to hunt for sustenance. Eventually, he gave up the rifle and manufactured his own bow and arrows, which he used to hunt deer.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gary_Paulsen  “Reading for me, is spending time with a friend. A book is a friend. You can never have too many.” — Gary Paulsen “Books make me feel safe. Books make me feel normal.” — Gary Paulsen
item.dc:creator: oukid

item.title: Meme dump to get you trough Friday #250 (hang in there, weekend is coming soon)
item.description:
1. A new villain arises
2. Fear meeee
3
5. "Goth Air"
6. Instincts
7. Yap
8. Well, that went as planned
9
10. Proof she has actual powers!
11. Willem Dafoe on being fan cast as The Joker
12. Snek life
13. When Miata owners see each other in the wild
14. Good thing she has quick reflexes
15. Thank you for upvoting this dump, if you did you are probably as hot as the hottest woman of 1995 - Tea Leoni! P.S. If this dump helped uplift your mood, please consider checking out my passion project - I'm working on a wacky game, inspired by The Stanley Parable, with lots of humor and wholesome moments. Please wishlist on Steam: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ Thank you for your kindness and have a great weekend!

item.title: Best of luck to you Romi
item.description: My name’s not Henry, and I’m not sure she’s Romi either, or just some scam program sending this shit out. I know I could ignore them, but part of me enjoys hearing them try to turn it into some weird “funny story how we met” type friendship, so I’ll what? Give them all my money? And another part of me wonders if they are real and being forced into working whatever this scam is. So, best of luck to you “Romi” if you need help, and fuck off if you don’t! https://humantraffickinghotline.org/en/contact
item.dc:creator: vant0037

item.dc:creator: ohherroeeyore

item.title: Oh shit it's my cake day mini dump
item.description:
Forgot it was cake day. Struggle bussed thru work with my lazy ass coworker. This was my mantra today. He still lives. For now.
But do we?
*at the end of an 84°F with 70% humidity day*
What my husband looks forward to every night we take our vitamins. The ones he bought are sour AF for some reason, and I always make this face when I take them.
Tell your cat I said
They scream for help, just like my knees
Bottle that shit Summer. And sell it.
The prospect is looking solid lately
Doesn't matter, will still be told it's all in my head
So not cool. Adding the PMS bus running me over was not appreciated this week.
.
Cat tax. This is Sooba. Not mine, but staying with me until his owner finds a new place.
Mister, also chilling here for the same reason
Theyre not good for much else atm
My son has discovered this truth. It was glorious.
Pick two. You can't have em all.
I'd like someone to toss me one today. It'd help.
Same Maisey...same.
Careful, hood go bonk
item.dc:creator: Apollymkatistrafia

item.title: Weight Loss check in
item.description:
I know, I know. Selfies are frowned upon. I'm just excited to have cheekbones after losing 80 lbs. It's been almost half a year since my first weight loss post and I wanted to post an update for personal accountability.
Honestly, this may be shallow is me but I think my favorite part has been shopping at Goodwill for my new size. I feel like it's opened a whole new world of fashion! Also, I believe people can dress how they want at any size. I was just too unhappy with myself to wear anything being stretch pants and oversized shirts.
How I wanted to dress on the left (notice I didn't leave the dressing room). How I dress now that I have some confidence back. (Peep the better hair too) Starting weight: 210 lbs Current weight: hovering around 130 lbs. Give or take few. If anyone wants to know, I did use Ozempic and Mounjarno. I was extremely lucky that my insurance covered it because I was prediabetic. Literally the only thing it changed was my appetite. I went from having binge eating disorder (BED) to setting reminders on my phone to eat. The forced change in appetite gave me the space to work through my BED. I feel like it will always be lurking around but I'm ahead of it now and my entire relationship with food has changed. Tl;dr-took Ozempic, lost 80 lbs
item.dc:creator: morganblue1988
